skills.name: Run a Container
skills.desc: Running a container involves starting a Docker container based on an image. It allows you to create and launch instances of applications in isolated environments.

skills.name: List Containers
skills.desc: Listing containers provides an overview of all Docker containers, including both running and stopped containers, on your system.

skills.name: List Running Containers
skills.desc: Listing running containers specifically provides information about the containers that are currently in an active state on your Docker system. This can be useful for monitoring and managing running instances.

skills.name: Start Container
skills.desc: Starting a container is the action of initializing a previously created Docker container, making it actively run.

skills.name: Stop Container
skills.desc: Stopping a container involves halting the execution of a running Docker container, allowing you to gracefully terminate its processes.

skills.name: Restart Container
skills.desc: Restarting a container means stopping and then immediately starting it again, which can be useful for applying configuration changes or resolving issues.

skills.name: Remove Container
skills.desc: Removing a container removes it from the system, freeing up resources. It's important for managing container lifecycles.

skills.name: Attach to Container
skills.desc: Attaching to a container allows you to interact with its processes in real-time, useful for debugging and monitoring.

skills.name: Execute Command in Container
skills.desc: Executing commands in a container lets you perform specific actions within the container's environment without accessing the host.

skills.name: View Container Logs
skills.desc: Viewing container logs provides insight into the output and behavior of processes running inside a container.

skills.name: Inspect Container
skills.desc: Inspecting a container provides detailed information about its configuration and settings.

skills.name: Display Running Processes in Container
skills.desc: Displaying running processes within a container helps monitor its activity and resource usage.

skills.name: Create Container
skills.desc: Creating a container involves defining its configuration and settings based on an image, preparing it for execution.

skills.name: List Container Ports
skills.desc: Listing container ports reveals the network ports exposed by a container, which is essential for networking and communication.

skills.name: Pull Image from Repository
skills.desc: Pulling an image from a repository downloads it from a remote registry, making it available for running containers.

skills.name: Tag an Image
skills.desc: Tagging an image allows you to assign a custom label to it, making it easier to identify and manage.

skills.name: Push Image to Repository
skills.desc: Pushing an image to a repository uploads it to a remote registry, making it accessible to others.

skills.name: Remove Image
skills.desc: Removing an image deletes it from the local system, freeing up storage space.

skills.name: List Images
skills.desc: Listing images provides an overview of the Docker images available on the local system.

skills.name: Search Images in Repository
skills.desc: Searching for images in a repository allows you to discover and explore available Docker images.

skills.name: Save Image
skills.desc: Docker save command is used to export a Docker image as a tar file, which can be shared or transferred to another system.

skills.name: Load Image
skills.desc: The Docker load command is used to import a Docker image from a tar file into the local Docker image repository.

skills.name: Copy Data Between Host and Container
skills.desc: Copying data between the host and containers is essential for sharing files and data between the Docker environment and the host system.

skills.name: Manage Volumes
skills.desc: Managing volumes involves creating, deleting, and inspecting Docker volumes, which are used for data storage.

skills.name: Display System-Wide Information
skills.desc: Displaying system-wide information provides an overview of the Docker system, including resources, containers, and networks.

skills.name: Show Docker Version
skills.desc: Showing the Docker version provides details about the installed Docker software, helping to ensure compatibility and track updates.

skills.name: Log into Docker Registry
skills.desc: Logging into a Docker registry is necessary to access private container images and repositories securely.

skills.name: Log out from Docker Registry
skills.desc: Logging out from a Docker registry terminates the authentication session, improving security when not actively using the registry.

skills.name: Manage Docker
skills.desc: Managing Docker involves tasks like starting and stopping the Docker daemon and configuring its behavior.

skills.name: Remove Unused Docker Objects
skills.desc: Removing unused Docker objects, such as stopped containers, dangling images, and unused volumes, helps reclaim disk space and maintain system efficiency.

skills.name: Manage Networks
skills.desc: Managing Docker networks allows you to configure network connectivity between containers and the host, enabling communication and isolation.

skills.name: Build Image from Dockerfile
skills.desc: Building an image from a Dockerfile involves following the instructions defined in the Dockerfile to create a customized Docker image, tailored to specific application requirements.
